member.name: M:Sharpy.Exports.Len(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISized)
member.summary: Return the length (the number of items) of an object. The argument may be a sequence (such as a string, bytes, tuple, list, or range) or a collection (such as a dictionary, set, or frozen set).

member.name: M:Sharpy.Exports.Repr(Sharpy.Object)
member.summary: Return a string containing a printable representation of an object.
member.remarks: By default, the representation is a string enclosed in angle brackets that contains the id of the object. A class can control what this function returns for its instances by overriding the method.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Exports.Repr(System.Object)
member.summary: Return a string containing a printable representation of the value type or struct. This essentially makes an attempt to return a string that would yield the same value when written in code.
member.remarks: This actually applies to all C# objects, but overload resolution should prefer the version for Sharpy objects.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Exports.Some``1(``0)
member.summary: Allows for code like var opt = Some(5); instead of var opt = new Optional<int>(5);.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ICollection`1
member.summary: Interface for sized iterable container classes.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IContainer`1
member.summary: Interface for classes that provide the method.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IContainer`1.__Contains__(`0)
member.summary: Called to implement membership test operators. Should return true if item is in self, false otherwise.
member.remarks: For mapping objects, this should consider the keys of the mapping rather than the values or the key-item pairs.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IContainer`1.Contains(`0)
member.remarks: In subclasses, this must call to correctly implement x in y behavior.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IItemsView`2
member.summary: Interface for mapping views over pairs of keys and values.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IIterable`1
member.summary: Interface for classes that provide the method.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IIterable`1.__Iter__
member.summary: Return an object. The object is required to support the iterator protocol described below.
member.remarks: If a container supports different types of iteration, additional methods can be provided to specifically request iterators for those iteration types. (An example of an object supporting multiple forms of iteration would be a tree structure which supports both breadth-first and depth-first traversal).

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IKeysView`1
member.summary: Interface for mapping views over keys.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2
member.summary: Interface for read-only mappings.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.Item(`0)
member.summary: Returns the value at the given key in the mapping. If the key does not exist then this raises a .
member.remarks: Should call underneath.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.__GetItem__(`0)
member.summary: Gets the value for the given key. Raises if the key doesn't exist in the mapping.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.__Or__(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping{`0,`1})
member.summary: Returns the union of this mapping and the other mapping, with conflicting keys using the value from the other mapping.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.__Or__(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IIterable{System.ValueTuple{`0,`1}})
member.summary: Returns the union of this mapping and the iterable of key-value tuples, with conflicting keys using the value from the other mapping.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.Keys
member.summary: Returns a view over the keys of this mapping, in insertion order.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.Items
member.summary: Returns a view over the items (key-value pairs) of this mapping, in insertion order.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.Values
member.summary: Returns a view over the values of this mapping, in insertion order.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.Get(`0)
member.summary: Gets the value with the given key, if it exists. If it doesn't exist, it returns .
member.remarks: Internally, this should call , unless the class is sealed, in which case it doesn't matter.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`2.Get(`0,`1)
member.summary: Gets the value with the given key, if it exists. If it doesn't exist, it returns the given default value.
member.remarks: Internally, this should call , unless the class is sealed, in which case it doesn't matter.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping`3
member.summary: Interface for read-only mappings as a curiously recursive template, with methods dealing directly with the given mapping type.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMappingView`1
member.summary: Interface for mapping views.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2
member.summary: Interface for mutable mappings.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.Item(`0)
member.summary: Gets the value of or sets the value of the given key.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.__SetItem__(`0,`1)
member.summary: Sets the given key to the given value.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.__DelItem__(`0)
member.summary: Deletes the value at the given key. Raises if the key does not exist in this mapping.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.__IOr__(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping{`0,`1})
member.summary: Updates this mapping with the pairs from the other mapping, with conflicting keys using the value from the other mapping.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.__IOr__(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IIterable{System.ValueTuple{`0,`1}})
member.summary: Updates this mapping with the key-value tuples in the iterable, with conflicting keys using the value from the iterable.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.Pop(`0)
member.summary: Removes the value at the given key. If the key does not exist, this raises a .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.Pop(`0,`1)
member.summary: Removes the value at the given key. If the key does not exist, this returns the given default value.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.PopItem(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes the first key-value pair from the mapping, by insertion order. If last is True, then returns the last such pair.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.Update(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMapping{`0,`1})
member.summary: Updates this mapping with the pairs from the other mapping, with conflicting keys using the value from the other mapping.
member.remarks: Internally should call if the class is not sealed.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.Update(Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IIterable{System.ValueTuple{`0,`1}})
member.summary: Updates this mapping with the key-value tuples in the iterable, with conflicting keys using the value from the iterable.
member.remarks: Internally should call if the class is not sealed.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`2.SetDefault(`0,`1)
member.summary: Returns the value for the given key, setting it to the given default if the key is not in the mapping, before returning it.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableMapping`3
member.summary: Interface for mutable mappings that can be updated with other mutable mappings via a curiously recursive template.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableSequence`1
member.summary: Interface for mutable sequences.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableSequence`1.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns or sets the element at the given index. Supports negative indices to get or set from the back. If the index is out of range, then this raises an .

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableSequence`1.Item(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the element at the given index. Supports negative indices to get or set from the back. If the index is out of range, then this raises an .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableSequence`1.Extend(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.remarks: Iterable<T> extends IEnumerable<T>.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IMutableSet`1
member.summary: Interface for mutable sets.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IReversible`1
member.summary: Interface for iterable classes that also provide the method.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IReversible`1.__Reversed__
member.summary: Called (if present) by the built-in to implement reverse iteration. It should return a new that iterates over all the objects in the container in reverse order.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1
member.summary: Interface for read-only sequences.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the item at the given index in the sequence. Supports negative indices to get or set from the back. If the index is out of range, then this raises an .

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.Item(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the sub-sequence at the given start and end (exclusive) indices in the sequence. Supports negative indices to count from the back. If either index is out of range, then this raises an .
member.remarks: Should call underneath.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.Item(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the sub-sequence at the given start and end (exclusive) indices in the sequence, skipping every step elements after the start. Supports negative indices to count from the back. If either index is out of range, then this raises an . If the step is less than 1, then an empty sequence is returned.
member.remarks: Should call underneath.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.Count(`0)
member.summary: Returns the number of times the given element appears in the sequence as evaluated by Sharpy's equality resolution.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.Index(`0,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the index of the first occurrence of the given element in the sequence as evaluated by Sharpy's equality resolution, within the given start and end (exclusive) indices. Supports negative indices to count from the back.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.__GetItem__(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the element at the given index in the sequence. Supports negative indices to get or set from the back. If the index is out of range, then this raises an .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`1.__GetItem__(Sharpy.Slice)
member.summary: Returns a (sub-)sequence for the given slice. If any component of the slice is out of range, then this raises an .

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`2
member.summary: Interface for read-only sequences as a curiously recursive template, with methods dealing directly with the given sequence type.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`2.Item(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the sub-sequence at the given start and end (exclusive) indices in the sequence. Supports negative indices to count from the back. If either index is out of range, then this raises an .
member.remarks: Should call underneath.

member.name: P:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`2.Item(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the sub-sequence at the given start and end (exclusive) indices in the sequence, skipping every step elements after the start. Supports negative indices to count from the back. If either index is out of range, then this raises an . If the step is less than 1, then an empty sequence is returned.
member.remarks: Should call underneath.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISequence`2.__GetItem__(Sharpy.Slice)
member.summary: Returns the sub-sequence at the given start and end (exclusive) indices in the sequence, skipping every step elements after the start. Supports negative indices to count from the back. If either index is out of range, then this raises an . If the step is less than 1, then an empty sequence is returned.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISet`1
member.summary: Interface for read-only sets.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISized
member.summary: Interface for classes that provide the method.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.ISized.__Len__
member.summary: Return the length (the number of items) of an object.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Collections.Interfaces.IValuesView`1
member.summary: Interface for mapping views over values.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Dict`2.Copy
member.summary: Return a shallow copy of the dictionary.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Dict`2.System#Collections#IEnumerable#GetEnumerator
member.summary: Delegate to specialized GetEnumerator() for generalized one.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IAddableWith`2
member.summary: An interface for a type (the augend) that can be augmented (or added to) with something (the addende) producing a sum (the result, which may be the modified augend itself.
member.typeparam: The addend (the increase or thing to add/insert).

member.name: T:Sharpy.IAddable`3
member.summary: An interface for a type (the augend) that can be augmented (or added to) with something (the addende) producing a sum (the result, which may be the modified augend itself. This version has native operator support.
member.typeparam: The augend (the thing to add something to, or the thing that is increased or augmented).
The addend (the increase or thing to add/insert).

member.name: T:Sharpy.IAddable`2
member.summary: An interface defining an augmentation operator, where the left operand is the element to be augmented (increased) and the right operand is the amount of augumentation or something to be included in the other element.
member.typeparam: The type that indicates the amount of augmentation or what is being included in the "augend".

member.name: T:Sharpy.IAddable`1
member.summary: An interface for a type that can be added to itself yielding a result of the same type.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IEquatable
member.summary: This interface defines objects that can be checked for equality via the __Eq__ method. Equality in Sharpy is not the same as reference equality (except for ). All Sharpy objects implement this interface. This is the non-generic version of the interface.

member.name: M:Sharpy.IEquatable.Equals(System.Object)
member.remarks: This should delegate to . All C# (and therefore Sharpy) objects declare this by existing because they all subclass which implements this.

member.name: M:Sharpy.IEquatable.__Eq__(System.Object)
member.remarks: This should delegate to when possible.

member.name: M:Sharpy.IEquatableWith`1.__Eq__(`0)
member.remarks: This defines equality between two objects of the same type.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IEquatable`1
member.summary: This interface defines objects that can be checked for equality via the __Eq__ method. All Sharpy objects implement this interface. This is the generic version of the interface.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IHashable
member.summary: This interface defines hashable objects whose hashes can be obtained via the __Hash__ method.

member.name: M:Sharpy.IHashable.__Hash__
member.summary: This method should return the hash of the object. In Sharpy, objects that have the same hash are not guaranteed to be equal.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IMultipliableWith`2
member.summary: An interface for a type (the multiplicand) that can be multiplied with something (the multiplier) producing a result (the product).

member.name: T:Sharpy.IMultipliable`3
member.summary: An interface for a type (the multiplicand) that can be multiplied with something (the multiplier) producing a result (the product) with native operator support.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IMultipliable`2
member.summary: An interface for a type (the multiplicand) that can be multiplied with something (the multiplier) producing a result (the product) with native operator support, where the result is of the same type as the multiplicand.
member.typeparam: The multiplicand (what is being multiplied), and also the type of the product (the result).

member.name: T:Sharpy.IMultipliable`1
member.summary: An interface for a type that can be multiplied by itself yielding a result of the same type.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Index.Normalize(System.Int32,System.UInt32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns a normalized index.
member.param: The maximum length of the given sequence
Whether this is for a slice or not.
Whether this is for insertion or not (allowing for past the end indices).
member.exception: If the given index is inherently out of range.

member.name: T:Sharpy.IndexError
member.summary: Raised when a sequence subscript is out of range. (Slice indices are silently truncated to fall in the allowed range).

member.name: M:Sharpy.IndexError.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Raised when a sequence subscript is out of range. (Slice indices are silently truncated to fall in the allowed range).

member.name: T:Sharpy.KeyComparer`2
member.summary: Delegator to statically chosen comparer.

member.name: M:Sharpy.KeyComparer`2.#ctor(System.Func{`0,`1})
member.summary: Delegator to statically chosen comparer.

member.name: M:Sharpy.KeyComparer`2.Compare(`0,`0)
member.remarks: Unlike in Python, this compares None to non-None values as ordering before the non-None value, whereas Python disallows such comparisons by raising a TypeError.

member.name: T:Sharpy.KeyComparerFactory`2
member.summary: Simplifies creation of a key comparer if the key is null, by returning the comparer for T rather than for TKey.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Operator.Exports.Eq``1(``0,``0)
member.summary: Compares two objects for equality.
member.returns: True if the objects are equal, false otherwise.
member.typeparam: The type of the objects being compared.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Iterator`1
member.summary: An object representing a stream of data. Repeated calls to the iterator’s method (or passing it to the built-in function ) return successive items in the stream. When no more data are available, a exception is raised instead. At this point, the iterator object is exhausted and any further calls to its method just raise again.
member.remarks: Iterators are required to have an method that returns the iterator object itself so every iterator is also iterable and may be used in most places where other iterables are accepted. One notable exception is code which attempts multiple iteration passes. A container object (such as a list) produces a fresh new iterator each time you pass it to the function or use it in a for loop. Attempting this with an iterator will just return the same exhausted iterator object used in the previous iteration pass, making it appear like an empty container.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Iterator`1.__Next__
member.summary: Return the next item from the iterator. If there are no further items, raises the exception.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Iterator`1.System#Collections#IEnumerable#GetEnumerator
member.remarks: Type-erased version of .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Iterator`1.__Iter__
member.summary: Return the iterator object itself (a shallow copy). This is required to allow both containers and iterators to be used with the for and in statements.

member.name: T:Sharpy.List`1
member.summary: A list of elements.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs an empty list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Constructs a list with a shallow copy of the elements in the iterable.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Add(`0)
member.remarks: For collection initializers. Also a part of the System.Collections.Generic.ICollection interface.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Copy
member.summary: Return a shallow copy of the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Sort(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sort the items of the list in place (the arguments can be used for sort customization, see Sorted() for their explanation).
member.remarks: This is not a stable sort.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Sort``1(System.Func{`0,``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sort the items of the list in place (the arguments can be used for sort customization, see Sorted() for their explanation).
member.remarks: This is not a stable sort.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.ToList
member.summary: Creates a shallow copy this list as a .NET list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.__Contains__(`0)
member.summary: Returns whether the item is in the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Contains(`0)
member.summary: Returns whether the item is in the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.System#Collections#IEnumerable#GetEnumerator
member.summary: Delegate to specialized GetEnumerator() for generalized one.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Append(`0)
member.summary: Add an item to the end of the list. Similar to a[len(a):] = [x].

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Extend(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Extend the list by appending all the items from the iterable. Similar to a[len(a):] = iterable.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Clear
member.summary: Remove all items from the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Insert(System.Int32,`0)
member.summary: Insert an item at a given position. The first argument is the index of the element before which to insert, so a.Insert(0, x) inserts at the front of the list, and a.Insert(Len(a), x) is equivalent to a.Append(x).

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Pop(System.Int32)
member.summary: Remove the item at the given position in the list, and return it. If no index is specified, a.Pop() removes and returns the last item in the list. It raises an IndexError if the list is empty or the index is outside the list range.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Remove(`0)
member.summary: Remove the first item from the list whose value is equal to x. It raises a ValueError if there is no such item.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Reverse
member.summary: Reverse the elements of the list in place.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Count(`0)
member.summary: Return the number of times x appears in the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.Index(`0,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Return zero-based index in the list of the first item whose value is equal to x. Raises a if there is no such item.
member.remarks: The optional arguments start and end are interpreted as in the slice notation and are used to limit the search to a particular subsequence of the list. The returned index is computed relative to the beginning of the full sequence rather than the start argument.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.__Len__
member.summary: Returns the number of items in the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.List`1.op_Equality(Sharpy.List{`0},Sharpy.List{`0})
member.remarks: This returns true for both lists if they contain the same elements, even if they are not the actual same list reference. If the elements are Sharpy Objects, then is used. Otherwise, is used.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Object
member.summary: Base class for all Sharpy objects (except value types), deriving from C# object.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.#ctor
member.remarks: Not publicly constructible.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.Equals(Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Not virtual to prevent subclasses from overriding this.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Bool__
member.remarks: Unlike Python where all base objects are implicitly truthy, Sharpy ones are not, to enforce custom implementations that are suitable for the given subclass.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Eq__(Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: By default, returns whether this and other refer to the same object via with a fallback to .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Hash__
member.remarks: By default, returns .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Id__
member.remarks: In Sharpy's reference implementation in C#, this returns the hashcode of the object by calling by default (not by ). This is because objects in C# are not guaranteed to have pinned memory addresses.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Ne__(Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: By default, inverts the result of .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Repr__
member.summary: By default, returns a string containing the result of in the form "<Object object with id {__Id__()}>".

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.__Str__
member.remarks: By default, calls .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.Equals(System.Object)
member.remarks: Sealed to prevent subclasses from overriding this mapping to which should be the one that subclasses override.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.GetHashCode
member.remarks: Sealed to prevent subclasses from overriding this mapping to which should be the one that subclasses override.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.ToString
member.remarks: Sealed to prevent subclasses from overriding this mapping to which should be the one that subclasses override.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_Equality(Sharpy.Object,Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Comparison between Objects is based on .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_Inequality(Sharpy.Object,Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Comparison between Objects is based on .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_Equality(Sharpy.Object,System.Object)
member.remarks: Comparison between Sharpy Objects and C# objects is based on which is false if the C# object is not a type-erased Sharpy Object. If it is one, then it uses .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_Inequality(Sharpy.Object,System.Object)
member.remarks: Comparison between Sharpy Objects and C# objects is based on which is false if the C# object is not a type-erased Sharpy Object. If it is one, then it uses .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_Equality(System.Object,Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Symmetrical equality prioritizing Sharpy Object's implementation.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_Inequality(System.Object,Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Symmetrical equality prioritizing Sharpy Object's implementation.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_True(Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Objects are truthy based on .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Object.op_False(Sharpy.Object)
member.remarks: Objects are falsey based on the inverse of .

member.name: M:Sharpy.Optional`1.__Eq__(System.Object)
member.remarks: Unlike other types, optionals are equivalent if they hold the same value.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Result`2.__Eq__(System.Object)
member.remarks: Unlike other types, optionals are equivalent if they hold the same value.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Set`1.Add(`0)
member.remarks: For initializer literals and part of System.Collections.Generic.ICollection interface.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Set`1.__ROr__(Sharpy.Set{`0})
member.remarks: For insertion order, the other set is iterated through first.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Set`1.op_Equality(Sharpy.Set{`0},Sharpy.Set{`0})
member.remarks: This returns true for both sets if they contain the same items, even if they are not the actual same set reference. If the items are Sharpy Objects, then is used. Otherwise, is used.

member.name: T:Sharpy.Str
member.remarks: Unlike Python strings, Sharpy strings use C# strings as storage, so they are always UTF-16.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.#ctor(System.Object)
member.remarks: calls so this implementation covers all native C# objects and Sharpy objects.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.op_Implicit(System.String)~Sharpy.Str
member.remarks: Implicit conversion from string.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.op_Implicit(Sharpy.Str)~System.String
member.remarks: Implicit conversion to string.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.GetHashCode
member.remarks: Same hash code as C# strings. Both are immutable and readonly with the same logical semantics.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.Count(Sharpy.Str)
member.summary: Return the number of times x appears in the list.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.Index(System.Char,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Return zero-based index in the list of the first item whose value is equal to x. Raises a if there is no such item.
member.remarks: The optional arguments start and end are interpreted as in the slice notation and are used to limit the search to a particular subsequence of the list. The returned index is computed relative to the beginning of the full sequence rather than the start argument.

member.name: M:Sharpy.Str.__Len__
member.summary: Returns the number of (Unicode) characters in the string.
